# Review: `deepmind/999.lean` ‚ÄĒ ErdŇĎs Problem 999 (Duffin‚ÄďSchaeffer Conjecture)

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

No issues.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

No issues.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 999`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/999`.
- [ ] **Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.**
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[KoMa20]` with full citation.

**Issue:** The module docstring contains only the title, reference link, and bibliography entry, but no plain-English description of the problem. The problem description appears only in the theorem docstring. The checklist requires a brief plain-English description in the module docstring itself (e.g., "Is it true that for any function $f$, almost all reals are approximable by coprime rationals $p/q$ within $f(q)/q$ infinitely often if and only if $\sum \varphi(q) f(q)/q$ diverges?").

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos999 ... end Erdos999`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos999`.
- [x] `open MeasureTheory Set` appears **before** the namespace block (line 28, before line 30).

No issues.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `DuffinSchafferApprox` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Not marked `noncomputable` ‚ÄĒ correct since the def returns a `Set` (Prop-valued), no classical choice needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] Name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `DuffinSchafferApprox`.
- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity check for the definition.

**Minor suggestion (optional per checklist):** Consider adding a `@[category test]` example to verify `DuffinSchafferApprox` behaves as expected on a simple case.

**Mathematical note:** The definition uses `f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē` (integer-valued approximation function), whereas the classical Duffin‚ÄďSchaeffer conjecture uses a real-valued function `f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with `f(q) ‚Č• 0`. Using `‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē` restricts to natural-number-valued functions, which is a strictly weaker formulation. This is not a checklist violation but is worth flagging for mathematical accuracy.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_999` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[KoMa20]`.

### `answer()` usage

The Duffin‚ÄďSchaeffer conjecture is a direct assertion (an equivalence statement), not a yes/no question or a "find the value" problem. This corresponds to **Case 3** ‚ÄĒ the theorem type is `P` directly with no `answer()` wrapper.

- [x] Theorem type is a direct Prop (no `answer()` wrapper) ‚ÄĒ correct for Case 3.
- [x] Quantifier `‚ąÄ f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē` is inside the theorem type, not a theorem parameter ‚ÄĒ consistent with quantifier placement rules.

No issues.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ‚ÄĒ a valid category.
- [x] Category is consistent with the proof body (proof uses `sorry`, and `research solved` allows `sorry`).

No issues.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number theory).

No issues.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` in the multi-line format (`:= by` on one line, `sorry` indented on the next). Acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

No issues.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

No variants present. Not applicable.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÄ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ą§`, `¬¨`, `‚ÜĒ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

No issues.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters not verified in this review.

Cannot confirm without running the build. No obvious issues that would cause build failures.

---

## Summary

**Checklist items failed: 1**

| # | Item | Status |
|---|------|--------|
| 3 | Module docstring includes plain-English problem description | **FAIL** |

**Suggestions (non-blocking):**

1. **Add a plain-English description to the module docstring.** For example: "The Duffin‚ÄďSchaeffer conjecture: for any function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$, almost every real number has infinitely many coprime rational approximations $p/q$ with $|\alpha - p/q| < f(q)/q$ if and only if $\sum \varphi(q) f(q) / q$ diverges."
2. **Consider using `f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ` with a nonnegativity hypothesis** instead of `f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē` to match the classical statement of the Duffin‚ÄďSchaeffer conjecture more faithfully.
3. **(Optional)** Add a `@[category test]` sanity check for `DuffinSchafferApprox`.

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 999
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/999](https://www.erdosproblems.com/999)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[KoMa20] Koukoulopoulos, D. and Maynard, J., _On the Duffin-Schaeffer conjecture_. Annals of
    25‚ÜíMathematics 192 (2020), 251-307.
    26‚Üí-/
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üíopen MeasureTheory Set
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üínamespace Erdos999
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí/--
    33‚ÜíThe set of coprime pairs $(p, q)$ with $q > 0$ that approximate $\alpha$ within $f(q)/q$.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üídef DuffinSchafferApprox (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) : Set (‚Ą§ √ó ‚Ąē) :=
    36‚Üí  {pq | 0 < pq.2 ‚ąß Nat.Coprime (Int.natAbs pq.1) pq.2 ‚ąß
    37‚Üí    |őĪ - (pq.1 : ‚ĄĚ) / (pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ)| < (f pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ) / (pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ)}
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/--
    40‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 999 (the Duffin-Schaeffer conjecture, now theorem):
    41‚ÜíFor any function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$, the property that for almost all
    42‚Üí$\alpha \in \mathbb{R}$, $|\alpha - p/q| < f(q)/q$ has infinitely many solutions with
    43‚Üí$\gcd(p,q) = 1$, is equivalent to the divergence of
    44‚Üí$\sum \varphi(q) \cdot f(q) / q$.
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíProved by Koukoulopoulos and Maynard [KoMa20].
    47‚Üí-/
    48‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    49‚Üítheorem erdos_999 :
    50‚Üí  ‚ąÄ f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē,
    51‚Üí    (‚ąÄŠĶź őĪ : ‚ĄĚ, (DuffinSchafferApprox f őĪ).Infinite) ‚ÜĒ
    52‚Üí    (¬¨ Summable fun q : ‚Ąē => (Nat.totient q : ‚ĄĚ) * (f q : ‚ĄĚ) / (q : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    53‚Üí  sorry
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üíend Erdos999
    56‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 999

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/999](https://www.erdosproblems.com/999)

[KoMa20] Koukoulopoulos, D. and Maynard, J., _On the Duffin-Schaeffer conjecture_. Annals of
Mathematics 192 (2020), 251-307.
-/

open MeasureTheory Set

namespace Erdos999

/--
The set of coprime pairs $(p, q)$ with $q > 0$ that approximate $\alpha$ within $f(q)/q$.
-/
def DuffinSchafferApprox (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) : Set (‚Ą§ √ó ‚Ąē) :=
  {pq | 0 < pq.2 ‚ąß Nat.Coprime (Int.natAbs pq.1) pq.2 ‚ąß
    |őĪ - (pq.1 : ‚ĄĚ) / (pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ)| < (f pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ) / (pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ)}

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 999 (the Duffin-Schaeffer conjecture, now theorem):
For any function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$, the property that for almost all
$\alpha \in \mathbb{R}$, $|\alpha - p/q| < f(q)/q$ has infinitely many solutions with
$\gcd(p,q) = 1$, is equivalent to the divergence of
$\sum \varphi(q) \cdot f(q) / q$.

Proved by Koukoulopoulos and Maynard [KoMa20].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_999 :
  ‚ąÄ f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē,
    (‚ąÄŠĶź őĪ : ‚ĄĚ, (DuffinSchafferApprox f őĪ).Infinite) ‚ÜĒ
    (¬¨ Summable fun q : ‚Ąē => (Nat.totient q : ‚ĄĚ) * (f q : ‚ĄĚ) / (q : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos999

input.new_string:
/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 999

The Duffin‚ÄďSchaeffer conjecture: for any function `f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē`, almost every real number has
infinitely many coprime rational approximations `p/q` with `|őĪ - p/q| < f(q)/q` if and only if
`‚ąĎ ŌÜ(q) f(q) / q` diverges. Proved by Koukoulopoulos and Maynard.

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/999](https://www.erdosproblems.com/999)

[KoMa20] Koukoulopoulos, D. and Maynard, J., _On the Duffin-Schaeffer conjecture_. Annals of
Mathematics 192 (2020), 251-307.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 999

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/999](https://www.erdosproblems.com/999)

[KoMa20] Koukoulopoulos, D. and Maynard, J., _On the Duffin-Schaeffer conjecture_. Annals of
Mathematics 192 (2020), 251-307.
-/

open MeasureTheory Set

namespace Erdos999

/--
The set of coprime pairs $(p, q)$ with $q > 0$ that approximate $\alpha$ within $f(q)/q$.
-/
def DuffinSchafferApprox (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) : Set (‚Ą§ √ó ‚Ąē) :=
  {pq | 0 < pq.2 ‚ąß Nat.Coprime (Int.natAbs pq.1) pq.2 ‚ąß
    |őĪ - (pq.1 : ‚ĄĚ) / (pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ)| < (f pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ) / (pq.2 : ‚ĄĚ)}

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 999 (the Duffin-Schaeffer conjecture, now theorem):
For any function $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$, the property that for almost all
$\alpha \in \mathbb{R}$, $|\alpha - p/q| < f(q)/q$ has infinitely many solutions with
$\gcd(p,q) = 1$, is equivalent to the divergence of
$\sum \varphi(q) \cdot f(q) / q$.

Proved by Koukoulopoulos and Maynard [KoMa20].
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_999 :
  ‚ąÄ f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē,
    (‚ąÄŠĶź őĪ : ‚ĄĚ, (DuffinSchafferApprox f őĪ).Infinite) ‚ÜĒ
    (¬¨ Summable fun q : ‚Ąē => (Nat.totient q : ‚ĄĚ) * (f q : ‚ĄĚ) / (q : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos999

lines: +The Duffin‚ÄďSchaeffer conjecture: for any function `f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē`, almost every real number has
+infinitely many coprime rational approximations `p/q` with `|őĪ - p/q| < f(q)/q` if and only if
+`‚ąĎ ŌÜ(q) f(q) / q` diverges. Proved by Koukoulopoulos and Maynard.
[KoMa20] Koukoulopoulos, D. and Maynard, J., _On the Duffin-Schaeffer conjecture_. Annals of
